title: Intensely Smoky Croatian Pork Grill
description: A dense, low-calorie-friendly Croatian dish that combines the richness of pork and tangy flavors from shaoxing wine, finished with a smoky smokiness.
instructions: Trim the pork shoulder of excess fat.
In a small bowl, mix together smoked paprika, garlic, and brown sugar (if using).
Rub the spice mixture all over the pork shoulder, making sure to coat it evenly.
Let the pork shoulder sit at room temperature for 30 minutes before smoking.
Place the pork shoulder in the smoker, fat side up.
Smoke the pork shoulder for 4 hours, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 160F (71C).
Remove the pork shoulder from the smoker and brush it with olive oil.
Increase the smoker temperature to 300F (150C) and continue to cook the pork shoulder for an additional 30 minutes, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 180F (82C).
Remove the pork shoulder from the heat and let it rest for 10 minutes before slicing it thinly against the grain.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve with your favorite sides.
To make the shaoxing wine glaze, combine shaoxing wine, smoked salt, and cayenne pepper in a small saucepan.
Bring the glaze to a boil over medium-high heat, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 5 minutes, or until the glaze has thickened slightly.
Brush the shaoxing wine glaze over the pork shoulder during the last 10 minutes of cooking.
To make the smoked paprika powder, grind the smoked paprika in a spice grinder or mortar and pestle until it reaches a fine powder.
Use the smoked paprika powder to coat the pork shoulder before serving.
